what_happened: Open questions rendered with **Q-001** bold format failed sync validation
why: validate_plan.py OPEN_Q_RE expects `- [ ] Q-001:` (plain ID, no bold) not `- [ ] **Q-001**:`
fix_applied: Removed ** bold markers from Q-### IDs in Open Questions section in both plan.md and the content field in plan.json
pattern_to_remember: Open question lines in plan content MUST use `- [ ] Q-001:` format (plain, no bold) to match OPEN_Q_RE = `^\s*- \[ \] (Q-\d{3}):`. Task lines use bold `**T-X.Y**` but question lines do not.

what_happened: Source plan.md lacked markdown heading markers (no # prefixes) - the entire plan was rendered as plain text
why: The source file was exported without proper markdown formatting, resulting in all headings appearing as plain text paragraphs
fix_applied: Added proper ## heading markers to all sections during normalization, mapped the existing structure to ClosedLoop required sections
pattern_to_remember: Source plans may be exported as plain text without markdown headings. Always normalize headings as part of import, adding # prefixes and ensuring all required sections (Summary, Acceptance Criteria, Architecture Fit, Architecture Decisions, Tasks, API & Data Impacts, Risks & Constraints, Test Plan, Rollback, Open Questions, Gaps, Visual References) are present with ## prefix.

what_happened: AC source fields in source plan referenced PRD section codes (PRD US-010 AC-010.1) but the import process requires normalizing these to 'Imported plan'
why: The import process specification states all source fields must be set to 'Imported plan' since there is no PRD section to cite in this import context
fix_applied: Replaced all PRD section references with 'Imported plan' in both the table content and the acceptanceCriteria JSON array
pattern_to_remember: During plan import, always normalize all AC source fields to 'Imported plan' regardless of what the source document contains (PRD refs, section numbers, etc.). The original source traceability is preserved in the criterion text itself.
